mentions.note: Apple's AI deal with Alphabet makes Google the default search engine and extends Gemini integration. Neutral to positive for Alphabet as a beneficiary of AI deals.

Equity issuance to fund unprofitable data-center buildout raises dilution fears. Though $920M/month SpaceX deal shows Google Cloud customers prefer Nvidia GPUs over internal TPUs—a bullish signal for Nvidia offset by equity-supply concerns.
Just completed multi-billion fund raise for AI. Supply wave will impact stock in near term as investors raise cash.
Successfully raised $45B secondary offering with Goldman Sachs underwriting; huge demand, few flippers; stock bought back $45B last year, now selling $15B—capital deployment shift.
Successfully raised $45B of $85B sought; Berkshire's $10B participation validates story. Stock briefly rallied post-pricing; smart to front-load capital raise before SpaceX/Anthropic/OpenAI deals.
Shock $80B capital raise signals data center buildout severely underestimated costs; vulnerability in AI capex arms race.

Multiple revenue streams: YouTube (largest video company), Waymo (number-one self-driving), Google/Gemini search, Google Cloud (best horse to bet on); higher-priced multiple justified by diversification.
AI leader with hardware integration potential; part of sooner-to-be-public AI titan cohort alongside Anthropic/OpenAI; thriving in new semis-dominant era.
FANG stock and Magnificent Seven member; dominates search and digital advertising; rate-proof and recession-resistant.
High-quality growth name (formerly Google) that performed well during recession; example of company that works in thick or thin economic cycles.
AI models layer company (Gemini). Massive infrastructure spending on AI buildout, competing with other model developers for GPU capacity.

Ensured years of colossal profits from data center capex. Anthropic $200B chip/cloud deal sends stock soaring. Google Cloud critical winner; Gemini AI expanding to Apple ecosystem (160M US iPhone users).
22% revenue growth, 82% EPS growth; Google Cloud up 63%; Gemini paid users up 40%; raising CapEx to $180-190B with clear ROI; coining money.
Crushed all line items; Google Cloud extraordinary, YouTube strong, search incredibly strong funding semiconductor buildout. May have own chip shortage.
Reporting Q1 earnings tomorrow (earnings D-Day); Cramer owns in trust; watch capex guidance; critical to break recent trend of mega-cap tech earnings leading to market downturns.
Reports Wednesday; massive data center investment at stake. Key question: are hyperscalers seeing AI returns or just burning cash? Answer drives sentiment.
Mosaic of search, Gemini, YouTube, Wemo, Google Cloud could deliver outstanding earnings; multiple growth drivers.
Caller bought at $275, up $60. Cramer: sellers will lose; headed to $400. Dual win on stock and Celtics (joking reference).
Gemini trapped in middle pack despite improvements; Cramer comparing to Google vs. Bing search—Claude dominance creates opportunity for Google if they pivot.
One of only three viable quantum computing players (with IBM, Honeywell); owns real quantum business; Cramer owns for travel trust.
Own for travel trust; quantum computing viable player; best exposure to quantum future.
Dinged last year, come all the way back; core search and cloud businesses remain competitive despite AI competition overhyped.
Eric's position; crushing it; data center, YouTube, diversified revenue; don't mess with it; one of absolute favorite oils.
AI user and beneficiary of hardware trend; part of 'king of creches' alongside Amazon and Meta in AI infrastructure buildout.

Bears rumored Google failing and Gemini slowing vs. Anthropic/ChatGPT; Cramer kept conviction (YouTube, Waymo, search, Chrome, Gemini); didn't dump; stock ramp 2%; no fundamental change justifies selling.
Okay day but underperforming broader data center narrative; $180B CapEx expected; free cash flow approaching zero next year.
Major Nvidia customer and cloud partner; first to market with Vera Rubin; brings customers into Google Cloud ecosystem via CUDA; strong engineering relationship with Nvidia.
Blew away numbers; winning AI race on consumer side; 175-185B capex vs expected 116.5B—best performing Mag 7 last year, due for breather; down 3% past couple days; buy more for Charitable Trust soon.
Only other Mag 7 stock beating market, up 68% YoY; beat and raise synonymous with company; Whimo raised $16B at $126B valuation; self-driving leadership.

Best stock we bought back for charitable trust; many wrote off as least of Magnificent Seven but turned out to be Silicon Queen; Gemini superior to other AI chatbots; YouTube and Waymo upside; reports Wednesday after-close.
Announced own chips but remains gigantic Nvidia customer. Demand from hyperscalers (Google, Amazon, Microsoft) enormous and growing — validates Nvidia's secular position.
Only Magnificent 7 stock currently strong; others temporary donors to storage plays; money will eventually flow back due to too many levers and smart management.
Gemini 3 now regarded as best consumer AI; solves search cannibalization concern; strong competitive position vs. OpenAI.
Broke free from mega-cap tech group in Q4 to soar; continued magical run, up 3% to start 2026; owns travel trust; only bright spot in mega-cap tech rotation.
Only clear AI winner at moment; partnered with Apple to power Siri; positioned to dominate generative AI landscape.

Up 65% in 2025 (best Mag 7 performer); antitrust fears proved overblown; Gemini 3 AI platform best-in-class; YouTube dominance, Waymo, Google Cloud strength, Apple search deal intact; 28x 2026 earnings reasonable; Cramer bought back for Chapel Trust, intends big position.
